questions.question_text: Why is the study of Software Engineering essential in modern computing? Discuss in terms of complexity, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.
A large software project is expected to span 2 years, involves 15 distributed teams, and has loosely defined requirements. Which process model is best suited? Justify.
Explain the steps involved in the Requirements Engineering process. How do these steps contribute to the success of a software project?

Read the following case description M/s Poornima Broker & Builders is a real estate company dealing with leasing the houses on rent. Their Real Estate Information System provide assistance to the agents as they sell houses. Interested Sellers/owners of the property contact the agency and an agent is assigned to help the seller to complete a Sales Request Form. Information about the house and lot taken from that form is stored on a computer file. Personal information about the sellers is copied by the agent onto a Sellers Personal Info Sheet and stored in a file. When a buyer contacts the agency, he/she fills out a buyer request form. On regular basis, the agency sends prospective buyers a home-on-sale magazine containing the addresses. Periodically, the agent will find a particular house that satisfies most or all of a specific buyer’s requirements, as indicated in the buyers requirement form distributed weekly to all agent. The agent occasionally photocopy a picture of the house along with vital data and send MLS Sheet to the potential buyer. When the buyer selects a house, he or she fills out a purchase agreement that is forwarded through the real estate agency to the seller, who responds with an offer acceptance or gives counteroffer After a purchase agreement has been accepted, the agency sends an appraisal report to an appraiser, who appraises the value of the house and lot. The agency also notifies its finance company with a finance request form Draw the Context Level DFD Draw First Level DFD
